name: mpilot

metadata.name: mpilot
metadata.summary: MPilot is a plugin-based, environmental modeling framework

documentation:
# MPilot
MPilot is a plugin-based, environmental modeling framework based on a bottom-up, many-to-many workflow that can be 
represented by a directed (not iterating) graph. MPilot is descended from the Environmental Evaluation Modeling System 
(EEMS), which was initially a fuzzy logic modeling package based on EMDS.

[MPilot Documentation](https://consbio.github.io/mpilot/)

# Installing

MPilot with EEMS can be installed with `pip`:

```bash
$ pip install mpilot
```

In order to run MPilot with NetCDF datasets, you'll need to install the NetCDF variant:

```bash
$ pip install mpilot[netcdf]
```

# Creating models
MPilot models are contained in "command files", using a simple scripting language. Here is an example model, which 
loads two columns of integer data from a CSV file, sums them, and writes the result to a second CSV file.

```text
A = EEMSRead(
    InFileName = "input.csv",
    InFieldName = "A",
    DataType = "Integer"
)
B = EEMSRead(
    InFileName = "input.csv",
    InFieldName = "B",
    DataType = "Integer"
)
APlusB = Sum(
    InFieldNames = [A, B]
)
Out = EEMSWrite(
    OutFileName = "output.csv",
    OutFieldNames = [A, B, APlusB]
)
```

# Running models

Models are run using the included `mpilot` program. The following commands will run a model using the EEMS CSV library 
and using the EEMS NetCDF library respectively:

```bash
$ mpilot eems-csv model.mpt
$ mpilot eems-netcdf model.mpt
```

functions.name: mpilot.utils.flatten
documentation: Flattens a list of lists of any depth to a 1D list and returns a generator

functions.name: mpilot.utils.insure_fuzzy
documentation: Limits all array values in-place to fuzzy_min and fuzzy_max and returns the array

functions.name: mpilot.utils.convert_eems2_commands
documentation: Converts command nodes returned by the parser to their MPilot equivalents

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.csv.io.EEMSRead
documentation: Reads a variable from a file

classes.name: mpilot.commands.CommandMeta
documentation: Command metaclass, used to automatically register commands, which can then be loaded by name.

classes.name: mpilot.program.Program

methods.name: mpilot.program.Program.load_commands
documentation: Discovers and loads commands from the given module and any sub modules. This method is mostly used internally.

methods.name: mpilot.program.Program.from_source
documentation: Creates a program from MPilot source code

methods.name: mpilot.program.Program.find_command_class
documentation: Looks up and returns a command class by name, or returns None if the command doesn't exist.

methods.name: mpilot.program.Program.add_command
documentation: Adds a command to the program.

methods.name: mpilot.program.Program.to_string
documentation: Returns a string with commands formatted in the MPilot command file syntax.

methods.name: mpilot.program.Program.to_file
documentation: Writes the program as an MPilot command file.
A program consists of connected MPilot commands, and the arguments that will be used to run them.

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.netcdf.io.EEMSRead
documentation: Reads a variable from a file, converting floats to nearest int when necessary.

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.netcdf.io.EEMSWrite
documentation: Writes one or more file

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.Copy
documentation: Copies the data from another field

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.AMinusB
documentation: Performs A - B

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.Sum
documentation: Sums input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.WeightedSum
documentation: Takes the weighted sum of input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.Multiply
documentation: Multiplies input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.ADividedByB
documentation: Performs A / B

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.Minimum
documentation: Takes the minimum input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.Maximum
documentation: Takes the maximum input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.Mean
documentation: Mean of input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.WeightedMean
documentation: Takes the weighted mean of input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.Normalize
documentation: Normalizes the data from another field to range (default 0:1)

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.NormalizeZScore
documentation: Converts input values into normalized values using linear interpolation based on Z Score

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.NormalizeCat
documentation: Converts integer input values into narmalized values based on user specification

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.NormalizeCurve
documentation: Converts input values into normalized values based on user-defined curve

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.NormalizeMeanToMid
documentation: Uses "NormalizeCurve" to create a non-linear transformation that is a good match for the input data

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.NormalizeCurveZScore
documentation: Converts input values into narmalized values based on user-defined curve

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.basic.PrintVars
documentation: Prints each variable in a list of variable names.

methods.name: mpilot.params.Parameter.clean
documentation: Clean and validate a raw parameter value

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.CommandDoesNotExist
documentation: The command file contains a command that could not be found in the command libraries.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.DuplicateResult
documentation: The same result name was used more than once.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.MissingParameters
documentation: A required command argument is missing.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.NoSuchParameter
documentation: An argument was provided that is not valid for the command.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.ParameterNotValid
documentation: The value is invalid for the given argument.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.PathDoesNotExist
documentation: The path is required to exist, but doesn't.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.InvalidRelativePath
documentation: A relative path was provided, but the program doesn't have a working directory

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.ResultDoesNotExist
documentation: A result was referenced which does not exist.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.ResultTypeNotValid
documentation: A specified result is not valid for the argument.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.ResultNotFuzzy
documentation: The parameter requires fuzzy data, but the specified result is non-fuzzy.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.ResultIsFuzzy
documentation: The parameter requires non-fuzzy data, but the specified result is fuzzy.

classes.name: mpilot.exceptions.RecursiveModelStructure
documentation: The model graph is contains circular references.

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.CvtToFuzzy
documentation: Converts input values into fuzzy values using linear interpolation

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.CvtToFuzzyZScore
documentation: Converts input values into fuzzy values using linear interpolation based on Z Score

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.CvtToFuzzyCat
documentation: Converts integer input values into fuzzy based on user specification

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.CvtToFuzzyCurve
documentation: Converts input values into fuzzy based on user-defined curve

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.CvtToFuzzyMeanToMid
documentation: Uses "CvtToFuzzyCurve" to create a non-linear transformation that is a good match for the input data

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.CvtToFuzzyCurveZScore
documentation: Converts input values into fuzzy based on user-defined curve

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.CvtToBinary
documentation:
Converts input values into binary 0 or 1 based on threshold.
Direction = LowToHigh for values below threshold to be false and above to be true.
Direction = HighToLow for values below threshold to be true and above to be false.

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.FuzzyUnion
documentation: Takes the fuzzy Union (mean) of fuzzy input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.FuzzyWeightedUnion
documentation: Takes the weighted fuzzy Union (mean) of fuzzy input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.FuzzySelectedUnion
documentation: Takes the fuzzy Union (mean) of N Truest or Falsest fuzzy input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.FuzzyOr
documentation: Takes the fuzzy Or (maximum) of fuzzy input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.FuzzyAnd
documentation: Takes the fuzzy And (minimum) of fuzzy input variables

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.FuzzyXOr
documentation: Computes Fuzzy XOr: Truest - (Truest - 2nd Truest) * (2nd Truest - full False)/(Truest - full False)

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.FuzzyNot
documentation: Reverses the sign of the input fuzzy values from positive to negative or negative to positive

classes.name: mpilot.libraries.eems.fuzzy.CvtFromFuzzy
documentation: Converts input fuzzy values into non-fuzzy values using linear interpolation
